0

Comments 0

No comments yet.

Select any text in the document. The "Add Comment" popup appears automatically (right-click also works). Comments stay here until the agent processes them. Use Copy all to send them to the agent, or Export as Portable (in the ... menu) to embed them into the file so they travel with it.

Kitchen-sink sample

A dense document used to stress the commentable-html anchoring. Select text anywhere and add a comment; it should anchor at any offset.

Inline element soup

Prose with ainline-codebemphasiscstrongd and adjacent nested spans here plus a real link right in the middle of the sentence, then more trailing text after it.

Short nodes: x y z and a lone Esc.

Entities & special characters

Ampersand &, less-than <, greater-than >, quote ", apostrophe ', non-breaking space, and a literal closing tag written as text: </script> and an HTML comment opener <!-- inside prose. None of these should break anchoring.

Unicode, emoji & direction

Accents: café, naïve, jalapeño. CJK: 日本語. Emoji (surrogate pairs): 🚀 rocket, 🦄 and a family 👨‍👩‍👧. Selecting across an emoji must not corrupt the offset.

RTL run: עברית מבחן and العربية mixed with English words.

Lists and a table

  • First bullet with a link inside it.
  • Second bullet, nested:
    1. alpha
    2. beta with code
  • Third bullet.
MetricBeforeAfter
p95 latency1.8s640ms
error rate2.1%0.3%

Blockquote and code

A quote with a nested

inner quote

and trailing text.

Select across these lines to test code-block comments:

def anchor(items, fn, acc=None):
    for x in items:            # comment with a </script> lookalike in text
        acc = fn(acc, x) if acc is not None else x
    return acc
plain preformatted block
  with leading spaces and a tab	here

Repeated phrases (occurrence anchoring)

Anchor target: this exact sentence repeats verbatim below.

Anchor target: this exact sentence repeats verbatim below.

Diagram (commentable nodes)

flowchart LR
    A([Source]) --> B[Ingest]
    B --> C{Valid?}
    C -->|yes| D[Enrich]
    C -->|no| E([Drop])
    D --> F[(Warehouse)]
